title: Side Quests List — Africa train, Brazilian Carnival, Antarctica, Patagonia w/ Huck
html:
Side Quests List — Africa train, Brazilian Carnival, Antarctica, Patagonia w/ Huck

Source: 2025-05-20 Apple Note. Five aspirational adventure bullets, written 1 week after the Comrades list and the day before the Austin move plan. Pre-graduation aspirational pivot.

TL;DR

  • The five side quests: ride a train across Africa, live amongst African villages, party in Carnival Brazil, go to Antarctica, visit Patagonia with Huck.
  • Carnival Brazil ties directly to the April 22 South America pricing scratch — same fantasy, one month earlier, with concrete dollar figures.
  • Patagonia with Huck names a specific comrade (Huck Reed from the Comrades list) — the only side-quest with a designated travel partner.
  • Antarctica is a non negotiables-style entry — bucket-list-grade, stands alone.
  • The list is a six month adventure creed-style frozen alternative timeline — these don't get pursued in the Austin / Rankett year that follows.

Key quote

"-Visit Patagonia with huck"

Pattern observed

This is the dreamier sibling of the Comrades list from 7 days earlier — same era, same audit instinct, but pointed at experiences instead of people. The pair forms one mid-May moment: who do I keep, what do I still want to do.

Africa appears twice (train + village living) — over-indexed vs. the other continents, suggests it's the deepest unscratched itch. Notably absent: anywhere in Asia (already done in the China chapter), Europe (never a Yahir target in the corpus), South America beyond Brazil (Cusco/Machu Picchu were already in earlier travel notes).

Open questions

  • Was this triggered by graduation, by reading something, or by an in-person conversation with Huck?
  • The Africa train — Tazara (Tanzania-Zambia)? Cape-to-Cairo? Yahir doesn't specify.
  • Does any side-quest item ever get pursued, or do all five sublimate into the Rankett grind?
